heading: Step 3: Set the expiration date.
body:
All dates are set to Timestamp in ISO 8601 date and time format in UTC (Coordinated Universal Time), in the `MM/DD/YYYY` format. Set the FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) expiration date
to be within four weeks of the date to which you pinned the FCV (Feature Compatibility Version). When the expiration date passes, the tooltip displays
`Expired XX/XX/XXXX`. Your FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) pin automatically expires after 4 weeks.

After the FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) pin expires, Atlas unpins your cluster's FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) on the next maintenance window starting on or after
the expiration date. If you already upgraded your cluster's MongoDB version, Atlas upgrades its FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) to match its
MongoDB version.

**Api:**

Atlas Administration API

To pin the FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) for your cluster using the Atlas Administration API, see
[Pin FCV for One Cluster from One Project](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-pingroupclusterfeaturecompatibilityversion).
